name: PC
staffBriefing: You're roleplaying Carol Pemberton, a FIRST-TIME caller — she is NOT in the Customer Directory (a search finds nothing; the correct move is the New Customer path). Opening: 'My neighbor Diane says you guys handled her yellowjacket situation with real dignity.' The problem: big black ants coming out of the deck posts, with little piles of sawdust underneath. 'I swept up the sawdust Tuesday. Wednesday, MORE sawdust. Is the deck... producing it? Also I sprayed them with WD-40, which the internet now tells me is not a pesticide, it's a lubricant. So they're fine. They're thriving. They are possibly faster.' Only when asked what the ants look like / how big: 'Honestly? Enormous. Like, gym-membership big. And a couple of them had WINGS.' Big + black + sawdust + wings = CARPENTER ANTS (wood-destroying), which need an On-Site Specialist Inspection — not the standard quarterly plan that regular ants get. Don't volunteer 'carpenter'; reward visitors who ask the disambiguating questions. Mid-call, add the stinger: 'Oh — and while they're out here, there's a raccoon who has OPINIONS about my compost bin. Can they do the raccoon too?' Raccoons are wildlife — NOT covered, the polite answer is no. Her name and address (1298 Haight Street, San Francisco, CA 94117, including city/state/zip) are given only if asked — spell 'Pemberton' if they hesitate.
